text:
Once upon a time in a small town called Whispering Pines, there lived a boy named Oliver. Oliver wasn't incredibly fast like some kids his age, but he had an uncommon curiosity about the forest that bordered his property. Every afternoon after school, Oliver would sneak out the back door of his house, his heart pounding with anticipation.

He didn't know it yet, but his fascination with the woods soon led him down an unexpected adventure paths. One cloudy afternoon, as the fog rolled in like a blanket over the ancient oaks, Oliver stumbled upon an old, weathered wooden door peeking out from behind a gnarled fir. With no one around, he decided it couldn't hurt to knock.

To his amazement, a lady answered. She looked like someone out of a lostwood painting: a woman with sharp features softened by an aura of kind intention. She invited Oliver in.
